name: Carbon dioxide production (VCO2)
description: CO2 production (VCO2) — volume of CO2 exhaled per minute, in L/min (absolute, not body-indexed).

concept.conceptName: Carbon dioxide production (VCO2) in Respiratory system

en.name: Carbon dioxide production (VCO2)
en.shortDescription: CO2 production (VCO2) — volume of CO2 exhaled per minute, in L/min (absolute, not body-indexed).
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Carbon dioxide production (VCO2) is the volume of carbon dioxide a patient's body produces and exhales per unit time, a marker of metabolic rate. It is measured by analysing the patient's exhaled gas, typically by a ventilator's volumetric capnography or a metabolic (indirect calorimetry) module. In source data it is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, reported in litres per minute (**L/min**) [1]. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia, mainly for ventilated patients and during indirect calorimetry.

## Included Concepts

This set contains the single LOINC concept for absolute carbon dioxide production:

- *21490580 / 60825-7 — Carbon dioxide production (VCO2) in Respiratory system (LOINC)*: VCO2 in L/min.

**Note on units**: reported in **`8698 / L/min — liter per minute` (UCUM)**, the recommended unit.

## Mapping Notes

- Map a recorded VCO2 value (in L/min, or mL/min after conversion) to *21490580 / 60825-7 — Carbon dioxide production (VCO2) in Respiratory system*.
- **Absolute vs indexed**: this set is the absolute VCO2 (L/min). Body-size–indexed variants (VCO2 per body surface area or per ideal body weight) have different units and are not part of this set.
- **Not the ventilatory equivalent**: the ventilatory equivalent for CO2 (minute volume divided by VCO2, used in exercise testing) is a different measurement and does not map here.

fr.name: Production de CO2 (VCO2)
fr.shortDescription: Production de CO2 (VCO2) — volume de CO2 expiré par minute, en L/min (absolu, non indexé).
